evals.prompt: How do I validate my email list in ZeroBounce?
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step guide for bulk email validation covering file upload, processing, status types, and credit usage

assertions.name: bulk_upload
assertions.description: Explains how to upload a CSV/TXT file for bulk validation, including file format requirements and column mapping

assertions.name: status_types
assertions.description: Describes the validation result statuses — valid, invalid, catch-all, spamtrap, abuse, do_not_mail, unknown — and what each means

assertions.name: credit_usage
assertions.description: Explains that each email validated costs 1 credit, with unknown results not consuming credits, and how to monitor credit balance

evals.prompt: How do I set up blacklist monitoring and DMARC in ZeroBounce?
evals.expected_output: Configuration guide for blacklist monitoring alerts and DMARC policy setup within ZeroBounce

assertions.name: blacklist_setup
assertions.description: Covers adding IPs and domains to the Blacklist Monitor, selecting which blacklists to track, and monitoring frequency

assertions.name: alert_config
assertions.description: Explains how to configure email or webhook alerts when a listing is detected, including notification preferences

assertions.name: dmarc_policy
assertions.description: Walks through setting up the DMARC Monitor with DNS record creation, policy levels (none, quarantine, reject), and report interpretation

evals.prompt: How do I integrate ZeroBounce real-time validation into my signup form using the API?
evals.expected_output: Technical integration guide for the ZeroBounce REST API v2 validate endpoint with authentication and response handling

assertions.name: validate_endpoint
assertions.description: Provides the GET /v2/validate endpoint URL with required parameters: api_key, email, and optional ip_address

assertions.name: auth_method
assertions.description: Explains API key authentication — passing the api_key as a query parameter and where to find/generate the key in the dashboard

assertions.name: response_handling
assertions.description: Describes key response fields (status, sub_status, free_email, did_you_mean) and how to branch logic based on status codes like valid, invalid, catch-all, and unknown
